author: jtraglia
content:
You just have to use my fork of assertoor & my deposit playbook:
```
assertoor_params:
  image: jtraglia/assertoor:invalid-sig-percent
  run_stability_check: false
  run_block_proposal_check: false
  tests:
    - file: "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jtraglia/kurtosis-devnets/refs/heads/master/assertoor-playbooks/builder-deposits.yml"
      config:
        totalDeposits: 40960
        perSlot: 8192
        startSlot: 33
        invalidSigPercent: 100
```

author: tuyen4818
content:
I suspect there is a bug in `onboard_builders_from_pending_deposits()`
the `validator_pubkeys` is only accumulated up to the current counter, and it has no idea about upcoming `PendingDeposits`
we want to make sure the current builder deposit is not conflicted to any pending validator deposits in the entire queue
in order to do that we need a separate loop for it, something like:
```python
for deposit in state.pending_deposits:
        if is_builder_withdrawal_credential(deposit.withdrawal_credentials):
            continue
        if is_valid_deposit_signature(
            deposit.pubkey, deposit.withdrawal_credentials, deposit.amount, deposit.signature
        ):
            validator_pubkeys.append(deposit.pubkey)
```

could someone double check? cc <@755590043632140352> <@592004585506340885>

author: tuyen4818
content:
a separate point, right now we eagerly validate signatures of all pending deposits which is very expensive
instead of that we can do it lazily
- have a for loop through `state.pending_deposits` to build a map like `Dict[Pubkey, PendingDeposit[]`
- in the main for loop, for each builder deposit, get `PendingDeposit[]` of the same pubkey
- if there are some, we loop through it and validate signatures

it'll achieve the same purpose but save us a lot of signature verification operations. But need a spec change for it because the result is we don't skip invalid signature deposits (unlike the current spec), but it's not the main intention of `onboard_builders_from_pending_deposits()` anyway

same trick could be applied for `process_deposit_request()` I think

author: jtraglia
content: <@575994543527297055> potuz is right. It's [here](https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/fa8bb08022199cfd59f8402c9ec12d9b7f8f6e5e/specs/gloas/fork.md?plain=1#L103).

author: jtraglia
content:
The spec does handle this & there are reference tests to enforce it:

* [`test_fork_builder_deposit_followed_by_non_builder_credentials`](https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/fa8bb08022199cfd59f8402c9ec12d9b7f8f6e5e/tests/core/pyspec/eth_consensus_specs/test/gloas/fork/test_gloas_fork_onboard_builders.py#L374)
* [`test_fork_validator_deposit_followed_by_builder_credentials`](https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/fa8bb08022199cfd59f8402c9ec12d9b7f8f6e5e/tests/core/pyspec/eth_consensus_specs/test/gloas/fork/test_gloas_fork_onboard_builders.py#L429)
* [`test_fork_invalid_validator_deposit_followed_by_builder_credentials`](https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/fa8bb08022199cfd59f8402c9ec12d9b7f8f6e5e/tests/core/pyspec/eth_consensus_specs/test/gloas/fork/test_gloas_fork_onboard_builders.py#L489)

Your suggestion is more of a design change. It would mean any validator deposit with the same creds would override the builder deposit (ie the validator would be created instead of the builder, even thought the builder was first).

author: pk910
content:
add this to the kurtosis config:
```yaml
dora_params:
  env:
    EXECUTIONINDEXER_ENABLED: "false"
```
that should reduce the load in dora for these mass deposit tests
